content.full_text:
Ravi Mehta
Chairman

Fair Political Practices Commission

July 19, 1995

Jerry Patterson, Esq.
Burke, Williams & Sorensen
3200 Park Center Drive, Suite 750
Costa Mesa, California 92626

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-95-222

Dear Mr. Patterson:

We respond to your request for advice under the Political
Reform Act (the "Act"). The Fair Political Practices Commission
is empowered by statute to provide advice to public officials and
their authorized representatives regarding their duties under the
Act. We do not provide general legal advice, nor do we interpret
local laws. Therefore, our response to your request is
necessarily limited and does not fully respond to the issues you
have raised.

You have requested advice on whether local contribution
limits apply in a recall election. A local jurisdiction may enact
additional campaign law requirements as long as those additional
requirements do not conflict with the Act. (Section 81013.) It
would appear that the local contribution limit referenced in your
request does not conflict with any provision of the Act.
Accordingly, a determination of whether or not such contribution
limits are applicable to a recall election is purely a matter of
interpretation of the local laws.

You have stated that the local laws incorporate by reference
the definitions established by the Act. We therefore confirm that
the current Commission interpretation of a recall election is that
such an election is deemed to be a ballot measure. (Burgess
Advice Letter, No. I-94-293.)

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 800, Sacramento, California 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. G-95-222
Page 2


     We hope that this response aids your analysis.  We regret
that we are unable to provide you with the advice you seek.


                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel
                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Daniel E. Muallem
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:DEM:ak

sections.question: Whether local contribution limits apply in a recall election.
sections.conclusion: A local jurisdiction may enact additional campaign law requirements as long as those additional requirements do not conflict with the Act. The local contribution limit does not appear to conflict with any provision of the Act. Therefore, whether such contribution limits apply to a recall election is a matter of interpretation of local laws. The Commission confirms that a recall election is deemed to be a ballot measure under current Commission interpretation.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether local contribution limits apply to recall elections and how the Political Reform Act's definitions affect this determination.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that local contribution limits do not conflict with the Political Reform Act and that whether they apply to recall elections depends on local law interpretation, while confirming that recall elections are treated as ballot measures under Commission interpretation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a formal FPPC advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. The FPPC declines to provide full legal interpretation, limiting response to Political Reform Act scope only. References prior advice letter No. 1-94-393.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether local contribution limits apply in a recall election.

CONCLUSION: A local jurisdiction may enact additional campaign law requirements as long as those additional requirements do not conflict with the Act. The local contribution limit does not appear to conflict with any provision of the Act. Therefore, whether such contribution limits apply to a recall election is a matter of interpretation of local laws. The Commission confirms that a recall election is deemed to be a ballot measure under current Commission interpretation.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that local contribution limits do not conflict with the Political Reform Act, and whether they apply to recall elections is a matter of local law interpretation. The Commission confirms that recall elections are deemed to be ballot measures.
